Independent Watchmaking A Global Catalogue
— makers listed
Curated by @Dante.Miami

Horology's Independent* Masters, Makers, Manufactures, and Micro-Brands

A living catalogue of independent watchmakers worldwide — those who operate outside the major brands and groups; who bring to their craft a pioneering spirit, an uncompromising vision, and a healthy dose of irreverence; who test the boundaries of the industry and push this art form forward with groundbreaking designs and technical advancements; who create timepieces with soul.

Specification:
Country:
Showing — watchmakers

A Note on Curation

This catalogue is dedicated to independent watchmaking — makers who set their own creative direction and answer to no distant parent company. To keep that focus meaningful, a number of well-known names are intentionally left out.

A brand does not appear here if any of the following is true: it is owned by one of the three global luxury conglomerates that dominate the watch industry; it is independently or family-owned but has grown into a large-scale commercial operation whose revenue or production volume places it closer to an established industrial house than an independent atelier; or its output consists primarily of homages — close copies of other brands' existing designs rather than original work.

See the full list of exclusions

Owned by a global luxury or watch group

Swatch Group:

Breguet, Harry Winston, Blancpain, Glashütte Original, Jaquet Droz, Omega, Longines, Rado, Union Glashütte, Tissot, Certina, Mido, Hamilton, Balmain, Swatch, Flik Flak

Richemont:

A. Lange & Söhne, IWC Schaffhausen, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Panerai, Piaget, Roger Dubuis, Vacheron Constantin, Baume & Mercier, Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, Buccellati, Montblanc, Dunhill

LVMH:

TAG Heuer, Hublot, Zenith, Bulgari, Louis Vuitton, Daniel Roth, Gérald Genta, L'Epée 1839, Chaumet, Fred

Franck Muller Watchland Group:

Franck Muller, Pierre Kunz, Backes & Strauss, Rodolphe, Alexis Barthelay, European Company Watch (ECW)

Fossil Group:

Fossil, Skagen, Michele, Zodiac, Relic

Movado Group:

Movado, Concord, Ebel, Olivia Burton, MVMT

Festina Group:

Festina, Lotus, Jaguar, Candino, Calypso, Perrelet, L. Leroy (considered for the exception above, but its flagship movement is built by an external Geneva supplier rather than in-house, so it does not currently qualify), Kronaby

Citychamp Watch & Jewellery Group:

Eterna, Rotary, Rossini, Ebohr, Codex, Dreyfuss & Co., J&T Windmills

Timex Group:

Timex, TX

Mondaine Group:

Mondaine, Luminox

Independently owned, but too large in revenue or production to be considered an independent atelier

Rolex, Tudor, Breitling, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet, Richard Mille, Seiko, Grand Seiko, Credor, Chopard (though Chopard-owned Ferdinand Berthoud is included — see below), Chanel, Hermès, Citizen (though Citizen-owned Arnold & Son and Angelus are included — see below), incl. Frédérique Constant, Alpina, and other Citizen-owned brands, Casio, Yema, Invicta, Spinnaker (owned by Dartmouth Brands)

Exceptions: included despite group ownership

A very small number of group-owned brands are included because they operate as genuinely autonomous manufactures — separate premises, separate teams, full creative and technical control over their own movements and designs, functioning in every practical sense like an independent house rather than a division of a larger commercial operation.

Arnold & Son and Angelus — owned by Citizen Group via Manufacture La Joux-Perret, but designing and producing their movements entirely in-house at their own La Chaux-de-Fonds manufacture.

Ferdinand Berthoud — owned by Chopard, but conceived and run as a dedicated independent manufacture in Fleurier with full creative and technical autonomy.

This list reflects our best current understanding of each brand's ownership and scale, and it will change as circumstances do — a brand that regains independence, or an independent that is acquired, will move accordingly. If you believe a listing here is outdated or incorrect, corrections are always welcome.
